title: Exporting dashboards into excel automatically
excerpt: Adding the ability to automatically export a dashboard as an excel file and then email the exported excel file to a specific user within our Monday account.

posts.author: DCGB (Michael Chase)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding the ability to automatically export a dashboard as an excel file and then email the exported excel file to a specific user within our Monday account.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @DCGB

I’m not sure if it is still relevant to you, but I came across your post and wanted to share it. Recently, we had a similar case from a client – they asked to make custom board snapshots in xls📷. I’ve described it here. I hope it will be helpful.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @DCGB, this is a really common reporting gap. If the goal is automated, scheduled Excel reports (not just PDFs), one workaround is using Time in Status for monday.com to generate structured time and performance reports that can be exported and shared on a recurring basis. It’s especially useful when dashboards aren’t flexible enough for downstream reporting or finance use.

There’s a 14-day free trial, and you can also book a quick demo to see how automated exports and reporting could fit your workflow.
